summary.intent: Address Codex review feedback on PR #704 in the ars-ui repo by fixing three review threads and pushing the resulting commit, then monitor CI and respond to any follow-up findings.
summary.outcome: All three original review threads were resolved with commit f599d408, CI passed, and a follow-up Codex finding about static unavailable dates not being checked during range validation was fixed with an additional regression test and committed.

repo: RangeCalendar has a `Context::unavailable_dates` field that drives `is_date_unavailable`, `aria-disabled`, and `data-ars-unavailable` attributes; static list must be wired through this context to affect all three.
Offset-aware triggers with outside-month duplicate cells must opt out of the roving tab stop to avoid sharing focus management with the canonical in-month cell.
Leptos and Dioxus RangeCalendar adapter prop specs must explicitly mirror `allow_single_date_range`, `min_range_days`, and `max_range_days` from core props.

Range validation had an early-return shortcut that skipped scanning static unavailable dates when a dynamic predicate existed; this was a latent bug requiring both sources to be checked independently.

code.finding: Range validation logic contained an early-return that bypassed static unavailable date checks when a dynamic predicate was present; fixed to only skip when both sources are absent.

Regression test `static_unavailable_dates_inside_range_block_validation` added to cover the case where static unavailable dates within a selected range should block validation even when no dynamic predicate exists.

workflow: Resolving GitHub PR review threads via GraphQL mutations allows combining reply and resolve in a single API call.
Triggering Codex re-review is done by posting `@codex review` as an issue comment, and review-in-progress state is indicated by an eyes reaction on the trigger comment.

CI pipeline has distinct fast lanes (format, snapshot labels, Clippy) and slow lanes (unit/feature/coverage/release/Dioxus feature flags); fast lanes complete in under 2 minutes, slow lanes take 5-10+ minutes.
Writing a failing regression test before applying the fix is the correct approach to validate that the test actually catches the bug.

friction: Long CI wait times required multiple sleep-and-poll cycles totaling ~10+ minutes before all checks completed.
Codex review introduced a follow-up finding mid-session after CI had already started, requiring an additional fix-test-commit cycle.
open_items: Codex review outcome on the final commit (fixing static unavailable range validation) was not yet confirmed in the transcript; review may still be in progress.
Full CI matrix result for the final commit was not yet available at transcript end.
